summary: Tested follow-up changes: simplified customization fields (name/familyName/photo only), post-order redirect to homepage, and WhatsApp message content. Updated backend pytest suite for new Customization schema (removed groomName/brideName/weddingDate/personalMessage refs). All 17 backend tests pass. Full E2E flow (customize -> checkout -> COD order and Razorpay-simulated order) verified via Playwright: redirect goes to '/' after WhatsApp tab opens, no Order ID badge shown, and wa.me message correctly contains Name/Family fields with no legacy fields.

integration_issues.issue: Occasionally hit a transient Next.js dev-server 'ChunkLoadError' on route transition to /checkout (known dev-server self-restart issue per review notes, not a real app bug). Retried once and flow worked correctly.

issues: Hero subtext still says 'Personalize luxurious wedding money covers with your names, sacred date, and a heartfelt message' - referencing 'sacred date' and 'heartfelt message' which no longer exist as customization fields (date/message were removed). Minor copy inconsistency, not a blocker.

action_items: Optional: update homepage hero copy to remove references to 'sacred date' and 'heartfelt message' since those customization fields were removed
No blocking issues found; all requested changes verified working correctly
critical_code_review_comments: models.py / server.py cleanly updated to new Customization schema (name, familyName, couplePhoto) with no leftover legacy fields
checkout/page.js buildWAMessage() correctly uses new field names and redirectToWhatsAppAndSuccess() correctly does router.push('/') instead of order-success
customize/[id]/page.js correctly has only 3 fields with live preview matching (no date badge, no message block)

/order-success route file still exists, loads without crashing when visited directly with invalid/test id (shows 'Loading order...' indefinitely since order not found - acceptable since it's no longer part of main flow)
seed_data_creation: Test orders/customizations created with TEST_ prefix via Playwright during E2E testing (not persisted as fixtures)
context_for_next_testing_agent: iteration_4 confirms: (1) customize page has exactly 3 fields (name/familyName/photo), no date/message fields anywhere; (2) full order flow (COD + Razorpay simulated) redirects to homepage '/' after WhatsApp tab opens, no Order ID badge shown; (3) wa.me message contains correct Name/Family/Photo fields with no legacy groomName/brideName/weddingDate/personalMessage; (4) backend pytest suite updated and all 17 tests pass. Only a cosmetic homepage copy inconsistency remains (mentions 'sacred date' and 'heartfelt message' which are no longer customization fields) - low priority.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - no bugs found in this iteration, only a transient dev-server chunk load error unrelated to app logic, and a minor homepage marketing copy inconsistency.
